opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I've got this awful tearing pain in my upper back that's spreading round to my stomach and left side, and my left leg feels cold and numb.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden severe tearing interscapular back pain radiating to the abdomen/flank, with left leg numbness.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden, about 45 minutes ago, instantly at maximum intensity
symptom_narrative.character: tearing/ripping sensation between the shoulder blades
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: worst pain I've ever had, 10 out of 10
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: upper back between the shoulder blades, radiating around to the abdomen and left flank
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: left leg feels cold, numb, and weak over the last 20 minutes
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: long history of high blood pressure, admits to often missing his blood pressure tablets, also smokes
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: unable to walk properly because of the leg symptoms, in severe distress

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: long-term smoker, poor blood pressure medication compliance, lives alone

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened, wants pain relief and reassurance

access_barriers.transport: lives alone, unable to walk normally, cannot safely self-transport
